seo.title: Enterprise SaaS AI Email Assistant AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for ai email assistant AI agents in enterprise saas. Covers SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR.

content.intro: Deploying an AI Email Assistant in an enterprise SaaS environment introduces significant compliance and operational risk, given the sensitivity of business communications, multi-tenant data isolation requirements, and contractual obligations under SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR, and CCPA. A misconfigured email agent can trigger SLA breaches, expose PII across tenant boundaries, or send unauthorized communications that result in contract termination and legal liability. This checklist provides concrete, actionable controls to ensure your AI Email Assistant operates safely, compliantly, and within the trust boundaries your enterprise customers demand.

sections.heading: Data Governance & Tenant Isolation
sections.description: Ensure all email content, metadata, and AI-generated drafts are strictly isolated per tenant and stored in compliance with contractual data residency requirements.

items.label: Enforce per-tenant data namespace isolation
items.description: Implement logical and physical separation of email data, embeddings, and AI context windows per tenant using dedicated encryption keys (per-tenant KMS keys) so no cross-tenant data leakage is possible during intent detection or draft generation.

items.label: Validate data residency on ingestion
items.description: At email ingestion, programmatically verify the tenant's contractual data residency region (e.g., EU, US, APAC) and route all processing, storage, and LLM inference calls to the approved geographic zone before any AI processing begins.

items.label: Implement attachment content sandboxing
items.description: Process email attachments in isolated, ephemeral sandboxes with no persistent storage; scan for malware and PII before passing content to the AI model, and purge sandbox state immediately after processing.

items.label: Define and enforce data retention policies per tenant
items.description: Configure automated deletion schedules for email content, AI-generated drafts, and processing logs that align with each tenant's contractual retention period, with cryptographic deletion verification for GDPR Article 17 compliance.

items.label: Restrict LLM context window to single-tenant data
items.description: Audit all prompt construction pipelines to confirm that retrieved context, few-shot examples, and RAG-retrieved documents are scoped exclusively to the active tenant's data store, preventing cross-tenant information leakage via prompt injection.

items.label: Document third-party AI subprocessor agreements
items.description: Ensure all LLM API providers used for email processing are listed as GDPR subprocessors in your DPA, with signed data processing agreements covering the specific email content categories being processed.

items.label: Implement tenant-scoped API rate limiting
items.description: Apply per-tenant rate limits on AI email processing endpoints to prevent one tenant's usage from degrading service for others, and to detect anomalous bulk email generation that could indicate account compromise.

sections.heading: PII Detection, Filtering & Content Safety
sections.description: Apply automated PII detection and content safety controls to all inbound email content and AI-generated drafts before they are presented to users or queued for sending.

items.label: Deploy pre-processing PII detection on inbound emails
items.description: Run all inbound email content through a PII detection pipeline (identifying SSNs, credit card numbers, health data, passport numbers) before passing to the AI model; redact or tokenize sensitive fields and log detection events for audit purposes.

items.label: Validate AI-generated draft content for PII leakage
items.description: After draft generation, apply a secondary PII scan to the AI output to detect hallucinated or inadvertently included sensitive data (e.g., fabricated account numbers, real names from training data) before surfacing the draft to the user.

items.label: Enforce tone and professionalism classification on drafts
items.description: Run generated email drafts through a tone classifier configured to flag drafts containing aggressive, discriminatory, or legally sensitive language (e.g., admissions of liability, regulatory commitments) and route flagged drafts to human review before display.

items.label: Implement confidentiality label enforcement
items.description: Detect confidentiality markers (e.g., 'CONFIDENTIAL', 'ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE', DLP classification tags) in source emails and block the AI from including confidential content in drafts addressed to external recipients outside the originating organization.

items.label: Block AI processing of legally privileged content
items.description: Integrate with your email DLP system to identify attorney-client privileged or regulatory-hold emails and exclude them from AI assistant processing entirely, logging the exclusion event for audit trail purposes.

items.label: Validate recipient email addresses against approved domains
items.description: Before any AI-assisted draft is queued for send approval, validate all recipient addresses against the tenant's approved external domain list and flag or block drafts addressed to competitors, regulators, or domains on the tenant's restricted list.

items.label: Scan attachments for sensitive data before AI summarization
items.description: Apply content inspection to attachments (PDFs, DOCX, XLSX) to detect financial data, health records, or contractual terms before passing attachment content to the AI for summarization or response generation.

sections.heading: Human-in-the-Loop Approval & Send Controls
sections.description: Ensure no AI-generated email is sent without explicit human approval, with configurable approval workflows that match the sensitivity and recipient profile of each email.

items.label: Enforce mandatory human approval before all sends
items.description: Architect the send workflow so the AI agent can only queue drafts for human review and cannot trigger the SMTP send action autonomously; the send action must require an explicit, authenticated user confirmation with a non-repudiation audit record.

items.label: Implement tiered approval workflows by recipient type
items.description: Configure escalating approval requirements based on recipient classification: internal recipients require single-user approval, external customers require manager co-approval, and regulatory bodies or legal contacts require legal team sign-off before send.

items.label: Display AI confidence scores and intent classification to approvers
items.description: Surface the AI's intent detection confidence score, detected email category (e.g., complaint, contract negotiation, support escalation), and any flagged risk signals directly in the approval UI so human reviewers can make informed decisions.

items.label: Implement approval timeout and draft expiration
items.description: Set configurable expiration windows on AI-generated drafts (e.g., 24 hours for standard, 4 hours for time-sensitive) after which drafts are automatically archived and cannot be sent without regeneration, preventing stale AI responses from being sent.

items.label: Log all human edits to AI-generated drafts
items.description: Capture a diff of all human modifications made to AI-generated drafts before send, storing the original AI output and final sent version separately in the audit log to enable post-hoc analysis of AI accuracy and human override patterns.

items.label: Provide one-click AI draft rejection with reason capture
items.description: Include a structured rejection workflow in the approval UI that captures the rejection reason (e.g., factually incorrect, wrong tone, wrong recipient) and feeds this signal back to your AI quality monitoring pipeline for continuous improvement.

items.label: Enforce send-time recipient re-validation
items.description: At the moment of send confirmation, re-validate all recipient addresses against current tenant blocklists and DLP policies, not just at draft generation time, to catch policy changes that occurred during the approval window.

sections.heading: Audit Logging, Monitoring & SLA Compliance
sections.description: Maintain immutable, comprehensive audit logs of all AI-assisted email actions and implement real-time monitoring to detect SLA-threatening failures, anomalous behavior, and compliance violations.

items.label: Implement immutable audit logs for all AI email actions
items.description: Write append-only, cryptographically signed audit log entries for every AI action: email ingestion, intent classification, draft generation, PII detection events, approval decisions, and sends. Store logs in a separate, tenant-isolated log store with WORM (Write Once Read Many) protection for SOC 2 and ISO 27001 evidence.

items.label: Log AI model version and prompt hash per email processed
items.description: Record the specific LLM model version, system prompt hash, and retrieval context identifiers used for each email processing event so you can reproduce AI behavior for any specific email during audits or incident investigations.

items.label: Define and instrument SLA breach detection alerts
items.description: Instrument real-time alerts for SLA-relevant metrics: email processing latency exceeding contractual thresholds, approval queue backlog exceeding defined limits, AI error rates above 1%, and send failures. Route alerts to on-call engineering with defined escalation paths.

items.label: Monitor for anomalous email volume and bulk send patterns
items.description: Implement behavioral anomaly detection on per-tenant email processing volume; alert and auto-suspend AI processing if a tenant's send rate exceeds 3x their 30-day baseline within a 1-hour window, which may indicate account compromise or prompt injection attack.

items.label: Provide tenant-accessible audit log export for GDPR compliance
items.description: Build a self-service audit log export feature allowing tenant administrators to download all AI-assisted email action logs for their organization in a machine-readable format (JSON/CSV) to support GDPR Article 30 records of processing activities and customer audit rights.

items.label: Conduct quarterly AI output quality audits
items.description: Sample 2% of AI-generated drafts per tenant per quarter for human quality review, measuring factual accuracy, tone appropriateness, PII leakage rate, and recipient correctness. Document findings and remediation actions as part of your SOC 2 continuous monitoring evidence.

items.label: Implement AI processing circuit breakers for downstream failures
items.description: Deploy circuit breakers that automatically fall back to human-only email handling if the AI processing pipeline error rate exceeds 5% in a 5-minute window, LLM API latency exceeds SLA thresholds, or PII detection service becomes unavailable, with automated customer notification.

items.label: Track and report AI-assisted vs. human-only email metrics per tenant
items.description: Maintain per-tenant dashboards showing the ratio of AI-assisted to human-only emails processed, average approval times, rejection rates, and PII detection events, making this data available to tenant admins for their own internal compliance reporting.

notes.note: AI-generated emails that constitute automated decisions affecting data subjects (e.g., contract offers, service terminations, complaint responses) may trigger GDPR Article 22 rights. Ensure your human approval workflow is documented as a meaningful human review step, not a rubber-stamp, and that data subjects can request human review of AI-assisted communications that affect their rights or interests.

notes.regulation: SOC 2 Type II — Availability & Confidentiality Trust Service Criteria
notes.note: Your AI email processing pipeline must be included in your SOC 2 system description and tested against Availability (A1) and Confidentiality (C1) criteria. Specifically, document how tenant data isolation, attachment sandboxing, and circuit breaker controls satisfy the confidentiality commitments in your system description, and ensure your auditor has access to AI processing logs as evidence.

notes.regulation: CCPA Section 1798.100 — Consumer Right to Know About Automated Processing
notes.note: If your enterprise SaaS customers use the AI Email Assistant to communicate with California consumers, your privacy policy and customer DPAs must disclose that AI is used to process and generate email communications. Ensure your CCPA privacy notice categories include 'inferences drawn from personal information' to cover AI intent classification outputs derived from consumer email content.

notes.regulation: ISO 27001 Annex A.8 — Asset Management & A.12.4 — Logging and Monitoring
notes.note: Under ISO 27001, all email content processed by the AI agent must be classified as an information asset with a designated owner and handling requirements documented in your asset register. Audit logs generated by the AI email pipeline must meet A.12.4 requirements for log protection, retention (minimum 1 year online, 3 years archived), and regular review, with evidence of log integrity verification included in your ISMS documentation.

cta.heading: Automate Your Enterprise SaaS Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your AI Email Assistant against these controls, generate audit-ready evidence for SOC 2 and ISO 27001 reviews, and receive real-time alerts when your deployment drifts out of compliance.
